prompt: The null check occurs after the dereference. What is the bug?
choices: s.length() returns -1 when s is null
s.length() is called before the null check; if s is null, NullPointerException is thrown before reaching the if statement
if (s != null) automatically prevents the earlier NPE
int len must be declared as Integer to handle null lengths
explanation: Execution is sequential. s is null. s.length() throws NullPointerException immediately — the if(s != null) check on the next line is never reached. Fix: check before dereferencing: if (s != null) { int len = s.length(); System.out.println(len); }
